title: Ensure public network access is disabled

input_row.title: Ensure public network access is disabled
input_row.description: Disable public network access to prevent exposure to the internet and reduce the risk of unauthorized access. Use private endpoints to securely manage access within trusted networks.
input_row.rationale: Disabling public network access improves security by ensuring that the service is not directly exposed to the public Internet. This has the added benefit of providing more granular control over security settings and configurations for those additional layers of separation.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services or Function App. 2. Click the name of a function app. 3. Under Settings, click Networking. 4. Under Inbound traffic configuration, ensure that Public network access is set to Disabled. 5. Repeat steps 1-4 for each function app. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list function apps: az functionapp list For each function app, run the following command to get the public network access setting: az functionapp show --resource-group --name --query "publicNetworkAccess" Ensure that the command returns "Disabled". Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 969ac98b-88a8-449f-883c-2e9adb123127 - Name: 'Function apps should disable public network access'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services or Function App. 2. Click the name of a function app. 3. Under Settings, click Networking. 4. Under Inbound traffic configuration, click the text next to Public network access. 5. Select the radio button next to Disabled. 6. Click Save. 7. Check the box to confirm the change. 8. Click Continue. 9. Repeat steps 1-8 for each function app requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each function app requiring remediation, run the following command to disable public network access: az resource update --resource-group --name --resource-type "Microsoft.Web/sites" --set properties.publicNetworkAccess=Disabled
input_row.impact: NOTE: Prior to disabling public network access, it is strongly recommended that, for each function app, either: • complete virtual network integration as described in "Ensure app is integrated with a virtual network" OR • set up private endpoints/links as described in "Ensure private endpoints are used to access App Service apps." Disabling public network access restricts direct access to the service. This enhances security but will require the configuration of a virtual network and/or private endpoints for any services or users needing access within trusted networks.
input_row.default_value: By default, public network access is enabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control checks if public network access is disabled for function apps. This is a standard security property related to access control over web services.
- AWS provides APIs through the AWS CLI and boto3 to access configurations related to services similar to Azure's function apps (like AWS Lambda). 
- The concept of preventing public network access can be correlated to AWS Lambda by checking if the function is associated with a VPC, which effectively removes public access.
- The configuration changes can be queried through AWS APIs, and there is no need for SSH/RDP and human judgment.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_lambda_function_public_network_access_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the `boto3` library to interact with AWS services.
- The specific method to use is `boto3.client('lambda')` to call `list_functions()` and then inspect the configurations.
- For each Lambda function, check the associated `VpcConfig` to determine if the function is configured within a VPC:
  - If `VpcConfig` is non-empty, public network access is effectively disabled.
- AWS CLI command to retrieve function configuration: 
  ```bash
  aws lambda get-function-configuration --function-name <function_name>
  ```
- The configuration property to validate is `VpcConfig`. Ensure that if it is not empty (i.e., it has a `SubnetIds` key), it means the function has restricted access and is running within a VPC, thereby disabling public access.
- **Pass Criteria:** If `VpcConfig` is present and has associated values, then it is compliant with the control of public network access being disabled.
- **Fail Criteria:** If `VpcConfig` is missing or empty, indicating the function could potentially be accessed publicly.

This comprehensive approach allows for a thorough and automated compliance check of public network access settings on AWS Lambda functions that aligns closely with the intent of the original Azure control.
